name: IBM Maximo Asset Management API
what_it_does: IBM Maximo Application Suite (MAS) REST APIs for enterprise asset management and CMMS platform. Enables AI agents to manage work order creation, scheduling, and lifecycle tracking for equipment maintenance operations, handle asset record management and equipment hierarchy data retrieval for asset lifecycle tracking, access preventive maintenance (PM) schedule generation and compliance tracking, retrieve inventory and spare parts management data for maintenance supply chain, manage safety permit and lockout/tagout workflow data for maintenance safety compliance, handle contractor and labor management data for maintenance workforce scheduling, access reliability engineering data including failure modes and FMEA for predictive maintenance, retrieve condition monitoring and IoT sensor integration data via Maximo Monitor, manage inspection and calibration record data for regulated equipment, and integrate asset data with ERP, IoT, GIS, and operational technology platforms.
use_cases: Creating work orders from IoT-triggered predictive maintenance agents
Tracking asset lifecycle from EAM portfolio management agents
Managing PM schedules from preventive maintenance automation agents
Optimizing inventory from maintenance MRO supply chain agents
not_for: Simple ticket systems without enterprise EAM and asset lifecycle management
Commercial facilities without industrial asset management complexity
Small organizations without IBM enterprise licensing capability
best_when: A utility, oil & gas operator, manufacturer, or government agency using IBM Maximo wants AI agents to automate work order management, predictive maintenance, asset lifecycle tracking, inventory optimization, and safety permit workflows for large asset-intensive operations.
avoid_when: SAFETY RISK: Maximo manages safety permits and lockout/tagout workflows for hazardous energy control — automated LOTO permit issuance without qualified person authorization creates OSHA 1910.147 violations and fatality risk. Nuclear utility Maximo deployments have NRC 10 CFR 50 regulatory requirements for work management. Automated critical asset work order closure without technician sign-off creates regulatory audit issues.

auth.notes: IBM Maximo Application Suite uses OAuth 2.0 (OIDC via OpenShift/IBM IAM). Maximo 7.6 legacy uses API key (MAXAUTH). MAS is OpenShift-based; REST API via OSLC and JSON API. Webhooks via Maximo Integration Framework (MIF) and MAS automation. Armonk, New York (IBM HQ). MAS 8.x on Red Hat OpenShift. Maximo 7.6 on-premise also widely deployed. Strong in utilities, oil & gas, government, and heavy industry. Competes with SAP PM, Infor EAM, and IFS for enterprise EAM.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — IBM subscription required
pricing.paid_tiers: IBM Maximo Application Suite subscription; module-based and AppPoint licensing
pricing.notes: Armonk, New York. IBM Corporation (IBM NYSE). Maximo acquired from MRO Software (2006). Maximo Application Suite (MAS) launched 2021 as cloud-native evolution. 50,000+ organizations globally. Strong in regulated industries: nuclear utilities, oil & gas, government. IBM MAS includes Manage (EAM), Monitor (IoT/AI), Predict (predictive maintenance), and Assist (mobile) modules. Competes with SAP EAM, Infor EAM, and IFS.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; documented per deployment

known_agent_gotchas: SAFETY RISK: Maximo safety permit and LOTO workflows for hazardous energy control require qualified person authorization; automated permit issuance without authorization creates OSHA 1910.147 violations
MAS 8.x vs. Maximo 7.6 API difference — MAS uses OAuth/OIDC and JSON API; legacy Maximo 7.6 uses MAXAUTH API key and OSLC/XML; confirm which version before integration
OSLC vs. REST — Maximo integration traditionally uses OSLC; REST API (JSON) is available but OSLC is more mature; verify REST endpoint availability for your use case
OpenShift deployment dependency — MAS runs on Red Hat OpenShift; API access may require OpenShift network configuration; on-premise deployments add network complexity
Integration Framework (MIF) — Maximo Integration Framework is the primary enterprise integration mechanism; REST is newer and may not cover all integration scenarios
Nuclear NRC compliance — Maximo in nuclear plant work management has NRC 10 CFR 50 Appendix B quality requirements; automated work order processing must maintain QA audit trail
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise asset management. SOC2, ISO27001, FedRAMP, NERC-CIP. OAuth2/OIDC. US/EU/on-premise. Asset maintenance, safety permit, and equipment data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise asset management. SOC2, FedRAMP, NERC-CIP. OAuth2. US/EU. Asset maintenance and safety permit data.
